summary: Excellent audience‑recorded tape of a late‑1984 New Year’s run, highlighted by a smoking China Cat → I Know You Rider and a powerful The Other One.
review: The show captures one of the strongest moments of the ’84 New Year's stretch – a blistering second set with a monster China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, an extended The Other One, and a heartfelt Day‑Tripper encore.  Reviewers rave about the thick, “air‑filled” audience recording that blends SBD clarity with crowd ambience, making it feel like you’re right in the arena.  While Garcia’s playing is noted as being in a rough spot, his leads are still impassioned and the band’s energy remains high.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently strong praise for both performance (standout songs, energetic second set) and recording quality (great AUD/matrix mix), outweighing a few neutral/negative comments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the tape as a reverse‑matrix mix – clear vocal/instrument separation with the thick, atmospheric crowd presence typical of AUD recordings.  No major dropouts reported; overall very listenable.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is the highlight, with a sustained jam vibe and strong transitions; first set solid but less frequently mentioned.  Energy stays high despite Garcia’s rough condition.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as being in “sad shape”/junkie heap, yet still delivering impassioned leads and thick guitar tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted singing on Touch of Grey with a new mic; otherwise not singled out.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s entrance into The Other One was called a bit quick, but overall rhythm work is solid.
band_member_comments.Drums: 84 drums/space described as thick, heavy and driving the jam sections.
